data.description: Investigate why a newly shipped Foolery knot was moved into Knots cold storage almost immediately. Concrete repro: /Users/cartine/foolery knot foolery-e8a2 shipped at 2026-05-30T04:15:31.523469211Z, 06:15 Europe/Stockholm. Direct lookup sees it: kno -C /Users/cartine/foolery show foolery-e8a2 --json and GET /api/beats/foolery-e8a2 with repo=/Users/cartine/foolery both show state shipped. List paths omit it: kno -C /Users/cartine/foolery ls -a --json, kno -C /Users/cartine/foolery ls -a --state shipped --json, GET /api/beats with repo=/Users/cartine/foolery, and GET /api/beats?scope=all. SQLite inspection showed foolery-e8a2 in .knots/cache/state.sqlite cold_catalog, not knot_hot; kno cold search e8a2 --json finds it. This made Foolery Retakes miss current shipped work because Retakes consumes the list endpoint backed by kno ls. Determine how and why a same-day shipped knot enters cold_catalog, whether this is intended compaction policy or a bug, and fix or document the behavior so recent terminal work remains list-visible or has an explicit list API that includes cold terminal records. Acceptance: root cause identified; regression test or reproducible check added; behavior fixed or deliberately documented with a Foolery-compatible API path; verify kno ls or an appropriate list command can surface recent shipped work such as this case.
